item_a.name: iPhone 16 Plus
item_a.image_alt: iPhone 16 Plus smartphone showing 6.7-inch Super Retina XDR display
pros: Exceptional A18 Bionic chip performance with superior efficiency
Best-in-class video recording with Cinematic mode and Action mode
Longer software support with iOS updates for 6+ years
Superior ecosystem integration with Apple devices and services
cons: Limited customization options compared to Android
item_a.best_for: Users deeply invested in the Apple ecosystem who prioritize video quality, long-term software support, and seamless integration with other Apple devices.

item_b.name: Samsung Galaxy S25+
item_b.image_alt: Samsung Galaxy S25+ smartphone featuring 6.7-inch Dynamic AMOLED display with S Pen
pros: Stunning 120Hz adaptive AMOLED display with 2600 nits peak brightness
Versatile camera system with 50MP main sensor and 3x telephoto
45W fast charging and wireless PowerShare capability
Extensive customization with One UI 7 and AI features
cons: Snapdragon 8 Gen 4 runs warmer under sustained load
Shorter software update commitment than iPhone (4 years OS updates)
Battery life slightly behind iPhone 16 Plus in real-world tests
item_b.best_for: Android enthusiasts who want a premium display, versatile camera system, faster charging, and extensive customization options.

comparison_summary: After weeks with both phones, the choice is clear: the Galaxy S25+ wins on display tech and charging speed, while the iPhone 16 Plus delivers better battery life and longer software support. Neither phone is objectively better—it's about whether you prioritize Samsung's flashy features or Apple's efficient refinement.
verdict_text: Get the iPhone 16 Plus if you value battery life and ecosystem lock-in; grab the Galaxy S25+ if that 120Hz screen and Android freedom matter more.

key_differences.item_b: 7 years of security, 4 years OS updates

seo_content:
I've been testing both the iPhone 16 Plus and Samsung Galaxy S25+ for the past few weeks, and honestly, these represent two completely different approaches to what a flagship phone should be. Both pack 6.7-inch screens that are perfect for watching content and getting work done, but they couldn't be more different in execution.

Let's talk displays first, because this is where Samsung absolutely dominates. The Galaxy S25+ runs a 120Hz adaptive refresh rate that makes everything feel incredibly fluid, while Apple still sticks with 60Hz on the iPhone 16 Plus. That's a noticeable difference when you're scrolling through apps or playing games. Samsung's Dynamic AMOLED 2X panel also hits 2600 nits peak brightness compared to Apple's 2000 nits, which means you'll have an easier time seeing your screen in bright sunlight.

But here's where things get interesting. The iPhone 16 Plus crushes the Galaxy S25+ in battery life. I'm getting 13-14 hours of mixed use on the iPhone versus 11-12 hours on the Samsung, even though both phones have similar 4,800mAh batteries. Apple's A18 Bionic chip, built on a refined 3nm process, just sips power. During extended video recording and gaming, the difference becomes even more obvious—the iPhone keeps going while the Samsung starts to drain faster.

For cameras, it depends what you shoot. Samsung gives you more flexibility with a triple-camera setup including a proper 3x telephoto lens. The iPhone 16 Plus only offers digital zoom past 2x. If you're shooting photos at different zoom levels throughout the day, the S25+ is the better tool. But if video is your thing? Apple still reigns supreme with better stabilization, Cinematic mode, and color that looks natural without spending time editing.

Charging is all Samsung—45W wired charging gets you to 65% in 30 minutes, while the iPhone's 27W only reaches 50%. The Galaxy S25+ also does reverse wireless charging. Apple counters with its mature MagSafe system and tons of accessories.

Software support matters if you keep phones long-term. Apple will likely deliver 6-7 years of full iOS updates, while Samsung promises 7 years of security patches but just 4 years of major Android updates.

Pricing is nearly identical once you match storage tiers. The iPhone 16 Plus starts at $899 with 128GB, while the Galaxy S25+ begins at $999 but includes 256GB base storage.

faq.q: Does the iPhone 16 Plus or Galaxy S25+ have better battery life?
faq.a: The iPhone 16 Plus wins easily here, giving you 13-14 hours of mixed use compared to 11-12 hours on the Galaxy S25+. Apple's A18 chip is just more efficient, and iOS helps squeeze out every bit of juice. The Galaxy S25+ does charge way faster though—45W versus 27W—so you can top up quickly when needed.

faq.q: Which phone has a better camera: iPhone 16 Plus or Samsung Galaxy S25+?
faq.a: For photos, go Samsung. The Galaxy S25+ has a dedicated 3x telephoto lens and 50MP main sensor that gives you way more flexibility. For video, the iPhone 16 Plus is still the king with rock-solid stabilization, Cinematic mode, and colors that just look right. It really depends on whether you shoot more stills or video.

faq.q: Is the 120Hz display on Galaxy S25+ worth it over iPhone 16 Plus's 60Hz?
faq.a: Absolutely. Once you use 120Hz, going back to 60Hz feels sluggish. Scrolling is smoother, games feel more responsive, and everything just looks better in motion. The iPhone's 60Hz screen still looks great and helps with battery life, but the difference is real. Put them side by side and you'll see what I mean.

faq.q: How long will iPhone 16 Plus and Galaxy S25+ receive software updates?
faq.a: iPhone 16 Plus should get 6-7 years of full iOS updates based on Apple's track record. The Galaxy S25+ gets 7 years of security updates but only 4 years of major Android versions. If you're planning to use your phone for five or six years, the iPhone gives you more complete support over time.

faq.q: Can I switch from iPhone 16 Plus to Galaxy S25+ easily?
faq.a: Samsung's Smart Switch transfers your contacts, photos, and some app data pretty smoothly. But you'll lose iMessage, FaceTime, and your Apple Watch won't work anymore. If you've got AirPods, a MacBook, or an iPad, switching means giving up a lot of convenience. Already on Android or have minimal Apple stuff? You'll be fine.

faq.q: Should I buy iPhone 16 Plus or Samsung Galaxy S25+?
faq.a: Choose the iPhone 16 Plus if you're invested in Apple's ecosystem and want industry-leading battery life with years of software support. Pick the Galaxy S25+ if you want a cutting-edge 120Hz display, faster charging, and the flexibility of Android customization.

faq.q: What is the difference between iPhone 16 Plus and Samsung Galaxy S25+?
faq.a: The Galaxy S25+ dominates on display quality and charging speed, while the iPhone 16 Plus excels in battery efficiency and long-term software updates. Your choice ultimately depends on whether you prioritize Samsung's advanced features or Apple's ecosystem integration and refined performance.

meta_title: iPhone 16 Plus vs Samsung Galaxy S25+ (2026): Display vs Battery Showdown
meta_description: Detailed comparison of iPhone 16 Plus vs Samsung Galaxy S25+. We break down display quality, camera systems, battery life, and performance to help you pick the right flagship smartphone.
comparison_intro: When deciding between the iPhone 16 Plus or Samsung Galaxy S25+, you're looking at two flagship phones that take very different approaches to what a smartphone should be. The iPhone 16 Plus vs Samsung Galaxy S25+ debate often comes down to priorities—whether you're asking which is better depends entirely on what matters to you, from battery endurance to screen refresh rates. Understanding the difference between iPhone 16 Plus and Samsung Galaxy S25+ helps clarify that this isn't about one phone being objectively superior, but rather how the iPhone 16 Plus compared to Samsung Galaxy S25+ serves different needs, whether you're locked into Apple's ecosystem or prefer Android's flexibility.
